All you do is take all the sex out, and use little short words, and little dumb ideas, and don't be too scary, and be sure there's a happy ending. Right? Nothing to it. Write down. Right on.

If you do all that, you might even write Jonathan Livingston Seagull and make twenty billion dollars and have every adult in America reading your book.

But you won't have every kid in America reading your book. They will look at it, and they will see straight through it, with their clear, cold, beady little eyes, and they will put it down, and they will go away. Kids will devour vast amounts of garbage (and it is good for them) but they are not like adults: they have not yet learned to eat plastic.

Dunkle Bestien umzingeln die Welt, während fünf humanoide Völker ums Überleben kämpfen. Als 13 Helden in den Süden entsandt werden, werden sie von Clevatess, einem Lord der dunklen Bestien, mühelos ausgelöscht. Von Hass auf die Menschheit getrieben, verwüstet er die Hauptstadt des Königreichs Hiden. Doch anstatt die Menschen endgültig zu vernichten, erweckt er die Heldin Alicia Glenfall wieder zum Leben. Sie soll ihm helfen, das Neugeborene Luna großzuziehen, deren Existenz das Schicksal der Menschen bestimmen könnte. Verkleidet in menschlicher Gestalt sucht Clevatess mit Alicia nach einer Amme, während Nachbarländer die geschwächte Nation ins Visier nehmen, um die einzige Waffenschmiede der Helden zu kontrollieren.

images.alt:
Title: 

Generative AI at the Intersection of Higher Education and the Workplace: Ethical Perspectives from Degree Apprentices 

Abstract:

Generative AI (GenAI) including Large Language Model (LLM) chatbots are now used extensively by students in higher education and by professionals in the workplace. Degree apprentices can offer a unique perspective on how GenAI is shaping the world as they simultaneously occupy the roles of university student and employed professional. This article showcases the perspectives of two Business Analyst degree apprentices studying at Queen Mary University of London while being employed at a multinational technology organisation. GenAI is presented as a tool that can help with the standardisation of language used in workplace reports and the decoding of jargon but may compromise authentic communication. Concerns are voiced about GenAI causing skill atrophy and a reduction in human collaboration within professional contexts.

- When debugging you can give access to logs and stuff to your model and create test cases from that, but in that case a big model is probably required
- Refactoring thousands of LoC also becomes possible with a big model
- Small model is ~$1/h, big model ~$10-100/h
- Gemini 3 Pro + Flash good combo

- Create a collection of skills for your workers. Not to reduce reptition, but as a catalog of things known to work well with AI
- Through skills, chunk jobs to MANY sub-agents
- Test skills on said small models
- Consumer-grade GPUs are close to being able to run these kinds of models.

That's literally my job right now

So far it looks like:

- Use OpenCode instead of locked-in solutions (open source, vendor agnostic)
- Use API providers directly, or OpenRouter (access to everything easily)
- Pick smaller models (Gemini Flash, Claude Haiku), they are pretty interchangeable

record.text: I might, out of curiosity. I'm fading it out, though, as it's not nearly as performant as Claude Code, so not too enthused on spending brain cycles on Copilot.  It's a sunk cost.

I have an account that was on the $100/year plan. It used to include Opus 4.6 at a 3x request cost. It is now gone, and shows up only under an "upgrade" option, along with 4.5, 4.7, and gpt-5.1/5.5.

So they're not honoring the previously available models for existing subscribers.

Not only have they increased the request cost for models, but if you were on a yearly subscription all the pricier models have disappeared — including Opus.

Github is doing its best to drive people elsewhere.

value.text: in fact the model pricing is interesting; big rises in costs for a couple of Anthropic models but dwarfed by the 18x rise in the cost of GPT‑5.4 mini

record.text: I am mystified at to where this "he learned his lesson" idea came from. It certainly wasn't a theme of his campaign.
